title: Bowling for Soup vs. Frank Turner & The Sleeping Souls
description: Two giants of the alternative scene are joining forces for one massive summer night in Cardiff – and they’re bringing special guests American Hi-Fi with them. Texan pop-punk icons Bowling For Soup and folk-punk powerhouse Frank Turner & The Sleeping Souls team up for the first time ever, bringing their Bowl My Bones UK tour to TK Maxx presents DEPOT Live at Cardiff Castle. Joining the bill are American Hi-Fi, celebrating 25 years as a band and making their long-awaited return to the UK. Best known for huge early-2000s hits like Flavor Of The Weak, The Art Of Losing and The Geeks Get The Girls, they add another layer of nostalgia and energy to an already stacked lineup. What started as an unlikely friendship between frontmen Jaret Reddick and Frank Turner has grown into years of collaboration, camaraderie, and fan demand for a joint tour. Now it’s finally happening – and Cardiff gets one of the biggest dates. Expect huge singalongs, feel-good chaos, heartfelt anthems and three back-to-back sets from artists who turn every show into a celebration. Bowling For Soup bring decades of hits, humour and high-energy pop-punk, Frank Turner arrives with an unmatched live reputation and his full band The Sleeping Souls, while American Hi-Fi deliver the kind of era-defining tracks that defined a generation. This is more than a double bill – it’s a full-throttle summer night built on community, connection and the joy of live music in one of the UK’s most iconic outdoor venues.
shortDescription: Bowling For Soup and Frank Turner & The Sleeping Souls bring their Bowl My Bones Tour to Cardiff Castle. Support from American Hi-Fi.
subcategory: Bowling for Soup vs. Frank Turner & The Sleeping Souls

title: Billy Ocean
description: With a career spanning over five decades, Billy Ocean has sold more than 30 million records worldwide and earned his place among the most celebrated voices in British pop and soul. Fans can look forward to classics including Caribbean Queen (No More Love On The Run), When The Going Gets Tough, The Tough Get Going, Red Light Spells Danger, Get Outta My Dreams, Get Into My Car and Love Really Hurts Without You – all performed with the unmistakable energy and charm that’s made Billy a true legend of the stage. Joining him in Cardiff is Marti Pellow, the unmistakable voice of Wet Wet Wet and one of the UK’s best-loved performers. Expect to sing along to Sweet Little Mystery, Goodnight Girl, Love Is All Around and more as Marti brings his signature mix of pop, soul, and showmanship to this summer spectacular. Set against the stunning backdrop of Cardiff Castle, this will be a night packed with nostalgia, feel-good anthems, and world-class performances from two of the most iconic voices in British music.
shortDescription: Pop and soul legend Billy Ocean headlines Cardiff Castle on 9 July 2026. With very special guest Marti Pellow.
subcategory: Billy Ocean

title: Indie Beer Fest Wales 2026
description: Wales’s independent brewing scene is thriving — and this summer, it’s all coming together under one roof. The Indie Beer Wales Festival 2026 is a brand-new three-day event taking place right here at DEPOT Cardiff, bringing together over 35 independent Welsh breweries for a weekend of discovery, conversation, and seriously great beer. Whether you’re a diehard cask aficionado or just starting to explore the world of craft beer, there’s something here for everyone. With 150+ beers on pour — keg and cask — you’ll have plenty of room to roam, taste, and find your new favourite. What’s included 150+ beers from 35+ independent Welsh breweries — keg and cask Live music & DJs throughout the weekend Street food to keep you fuelled all day A pint glass + beer token included with every ticket to get you started Three days. Three sessions. One unforgettable celebration of Welsh brewing culture. Come for a session, stay for the weekend — however you enjoy it, this is a summer event you won’t want to miss.
shortDescription: The first ever celebration of independent Welsh brewing — 150+ beers, live music & street food. Three days. One roof.
subcategory: Indie Beer Fest Wales 2026

title: Bastille with Arthur Hill + Sofia Camara
description: Bastille head to Cardiff Castle in July for a huge outdoor headline show in the heart of the Welsh capital. One of the UK’s biggest and most loved bands of the last decade, Bastille have built a global following with their soaring alt-pop sound, emotional songwriting and unforgettable live performances. From the breakout success of Pompeii to fan favourites like Things We Lost In The Fire, Good Grief and Happier, the band’s catalogue has soundtracked a generation. Fronted by Dan Smith, Bastille have earned multiple BRIT nominations, topped charts around the world and amassed billions of streams across their career. Their powerful mix of cinematic production, anthemic choruses and introspective lyrics has made them one of the defining British bands of modern pop. Renowned for their electric live shows, Bastille bring an atmosphere that turns every performance into a shared moment with the crowd — huge singalongs, emotional highs and festival-sized energy from start to finish. Joining them for this special Cardiff Castle show are Arthur Hill and rising pop star Sofia Camara, making this one of the standout nights of the summer. Set within the historic walls of Cardiff Castle, this open-air show promises an unforgettable evening of anthems, atmosphere and one of the UK’s biggest live acts.
shortDescription: Bastille headline Cardiff Castle with support from Arthur Hill and Sofia Camara. Experience them live in the heart of Cardiff.
subcategory: Bastille with Arthur Hill + Sofia Camara

title: David Gray – Past & Present Tour
description: With special guest The Divine Comedy Following a run of 68 sold-out shows across the USA, Australia, UK and Ireland in 2025, multi-platinum artist David Gray has announced a brand-new summer date in Cardiff as part of his Past & Present World Tour. On Saturday 11th July 2026, Cardiff Castle will host the closing show of Gray’s UK & Ireland run – a night set to be nothing short of spectacular. Joining him will be very special guest The Divine Comedy, bringing an extra layer of magic to this one-off evening. The tour has already taken in legendary venues such as London’s Royal Albert Hall, Glasgow’s SEC Armadillo, Manchester’s O2 Apollo and Dublin’s 3Arena, as well as a celebrated headline slot at Electric Picnic. His latest album Dear Life has been praised as some of his most powerful and emotive work since White Ladder, showcasing Gray’s enduring artistry as one of the UK’s most influential singer-songwriters. Don’t miss your chance to experience an unforgettable night of music in the iconic surroundings of Cardiff Castle. Tickets: Presale: Thursday 4th September, 10am General Sale: Friday 5th September, 10am
shortDescription: David Gray brings his Past & Present Tour to Cardiff Castle, Sat 11 July 2026, with special guest The Divine Comedy.

title: Richard Ashcroft
description: Richard Ashcroft heads to Cardiff Castle next summer for a huge outdoor headline show on Thursday 16 July. Fresh off a standout year supporting Oasis across their 2025 stadium dates and the success of his seventh solo album Lovin You — which landed at Number Three in the UK charts — he returns in peak form. A two-time Ivor Novello and triple BRIT Award winner, Ashcroft remains one of the UK’s most influential songwriters. His set will draw from a catalogue that spans generational anthems with The Verve through to his expansive solo work. He’s joined by a heavyweight support bill: original Kasabian frontman Tom Meighan, bringing his trademark vocals and material from his solo album The Reckoning, plus rising indie favourites Apollo Junction opening the night. This show joins a stacked summer of music at TK Maxx presents DEPOT Live at Cardiff Castle — one of 2026’s major highlights. 🎟 Tickets Presale opens Thursday 27 November, 10AM General sale from Friday 28 November, 10AM

title: Curated by Sammy Virji
description: UK Garage’s biggest breakout star Sammy Virji lands in Cardiff for a huge open-air headline show at Shangri-La in the Castle, joined by a stacked line-up of special guests including Salute, Silva Bumpa, Arthi B2B Saint Ludo, Yemz and Lucas Alexander. One of the most in-demand names in dance music right now, Virji is at the forefront of the UKG resurgence. From viral DJ Mag sets to packed-out festival crowds worldwide, his rise has been rapid. Known for feel-good energy, heavy basslines and razor-sharp selections, his sets are built for full-scale party moments. Joining the bill is Vienna-born producer Salute, alongside fast-rising UK talent Silva Bumpa, while Arthi B2B Saint Ludo, Yemz and Lucas Alexander round out a huge line-up of club innovators and emerging names. In recent years, Virji has gone B2B with Four Tet, delivered a standout BBC Radio 1 Essential Mix and picked up DJ Mag Best Producer at the 2025 Best of British Awards. His tracks — including If U Need It and Cops & Robbers with Skepta — have racked up tens of millions of streams worldwide. TICKETS ➡️ On sale from Tuesday 10 March, 10AM
shortDescription: Sammy Virji headlines with Salute, Silva Bumpa, Arthi B2B Saint Ludo, Yemz and Lucas Alexander for a huge edition of Shangri-La in the Castle
subcategory: Curated by Sammy Virji

title: Self Esteem
description: Self Esteem returns to Cardiff next summer for a major outdoor headline show at Cardiff Castle – her biggest Welsh date to date. The Ivor Novello Award-winning singer, songwriter and actor brings the next chapter of A Complicated Woman to the Welsh capital, joined by special guest Kae Tempest and opening support Joshua Idehen. Fresh off the release of her acclaimed third album and debut book, Self Esteem (Rebecca Lucy Taylor) enters 2026 at full throttle. From the anthemic Focus Is Power – soundtrack to the Lionesses’ Euros victory coverage – to her run as Sally Bowles in Cabaret, Taylor has become one of the UK’s most fearless and celebrated creative voices. Since breaking through with Prioritise Pleasure, she’s earned Mercury, BRIT and NME nominations, racked up cross-station BBC playlisting, and cemented herself as one of the most compelling performers in modern pop. Joining her in Cardiff is Kae Tempest – the revered poet, playwright and recording artist whose career spans award-winning theatre, Mercury-nominated albums and internationally acclaimed written works. Opening the night is Joshua Idehen, the British-Nigerian poet and artist whose viral track Mum Does The Washing sparked a new wave of attention for his powerful spoken word and genre-blurring live performances. Presented by DEPOT Live, Cuffe & Taylor and Clwb Ifor Bach.
shortDescription: Self Esteem brings a headline show with special guest Kae Tempest, and Joshua Idehen to Cardiff Castle on Friday 24th July 2026
subcategory: Self Esteem

title: Depot In The Castle 2026: The Wombats, Sugababes +More
description: Depot in the Castle returns to the iconic grounds of Cardiff Castle on Saturday 25 July 2026 for a huge all-day celebration of live music, street food and entertainment – with The Wombats confirmed as headliners and Sugababes announced as special guests. One of Wales’ best-loved family-friendly festivals, Depot in the Castle brings together major headline acts, rising talent and a buzzing summer festival atmosphere right in the heart of the city. Fresh from the release of their sixth studio album Oh! The Ocean, The Wombats remain one of the UK’s most enduring indie bands, known for era-defining anthems including Let’s Dance to Joy Division, Greek Tragedy and Moving to New York. Expect a huge sing-along set to close out the day. Joining them are pop icons Sugababes, bringing their unmistakable vocals and a catalogue packed with hits such as Overload, Round Round, Freak Like Me and Push The Button. Following their biggest UK arena tour to date, they arrive in Cardiff at the peak of their powers. The full line-up also features: Red Rum Club – delivering their trademark mariachi-tinged indie rock Ben Ellis – a fast-rising Cardiff talent Afro Cluster – bringing high-energy Afrobeat and funk The Family Battenberg – known for their energetic live shows Alongside the music, expect local street food traders, family-friendly activities and a vibrant festival atmosphere throughout the day. Following last year’s sold-out edition headlined by Jess Glynne, Depot in the Castle 2026 is shaping up to be one of the standout events of the summer.
shortDescription: Join The Wombats, Sugababes and more at Depot in the Castle 2026 – Cardiff’s ultimate one-day festival at Cardiff Castle.
subcategory: Depot In The Castle 2026: The Wombats, Sugababes +More

title: SOLD OUT: David Byrne
description: David Byrne brings his Who Is The Sky? World Tour to Cardiff Castle this summer, following a huge sold-out arena show in the city. One of the most influential and inventive artists of his generation, Byrne’s career spans decades — from co-founding Talking Heads to an acclaimed solo catalogue and groundbreaking work across music, theatre, film and visual art. His live shows are renowned for their creativity, energy and emotional impact. The Who Is The Sky? tour builds on the spirit of American Utopia, blending bold visuals, cinematic arrangements and infectious rhythm with songs drawn from across Byrne’s career — including Talking Heads classics, solo favourites and material from his latest album Who Is The Sky?. Joining David Byrne as special guest support is Anna Calvi. A three-time Mercury Prize nominee and BRIT Award nominee, Calvi is celebrated for her powerful, uncompromising sound and electrifying live performances. Her latest album Hunter is a fearless and visceral release, and she recently composed the score for the final two seasons of Peaky Blinders. Set against the dramatic backdrop of Cardiff Castle, this open-air summer show promises an unforgettable night — bringing together two truly distinctive artists on one iconic stage. 🎟 Tickets Presale opens Thursday 5 February, 10AM General sale from Friday 6 February, 10AM
shortDescription: David Byrne brings his Who Is The Sky? World Tour to Cardiff Castle, July 25th with special guest Anna Calvi.
subcategory: SOLD OUT: David Byrne

title: The Flaming Lips
description: One of the most inventive and influential live bands of the last three decades, The Flaming Lips return to Cardiff for a very special An Evening With performance at DEPOT. Renowned for their genre-defying sound, surreal visuals and euphoric live shows, the band will perform two unique sets on the night. Offering fans a deeper, more expansive journey through their celebrated catalogue. Expect moments of psychedelic beauty, towering anthems and the unmistakable sense of spectacle that has made their live shows legendary worldwide. From era-defining albums to left-field experiments and fan-favourite singalongs, this will be an immersive, one-off experience from a band that continues to push the boundaries of what a live show can be. 🎟️ TICKETS Presale – Wednesday, 11 Feb 10AM General sale – Friday, 13 Feb 10AM
shortDescription: The Flaming Lips return to Cardiff for An Evening With at DEPOT, performing two unique live sets on 29 July.
subcategory: The Flaming Lips

title: Biffy Clyro
description: Scottish rock icons Biffy Clyro return to Wales this summer to headline a huge outdoor show at Cardiff Castle as part of TK Maxx presents DEPOT Live at Cardiff Castle. Following their hugely successful Cardiff date on The Futique Tour, the trio return by popular demand for one of the standout live shows of the summer. Renowned for their explosive performances and emotionally charged anthems, Biffy Clyro have built a reputation as one of the UK’s most powerful and reliable live bands. Expect a career-spanning set packed with era-defining favourites alongside material from their celebrated catalogue, delivered with the intensity and precision that has seen them headline the world’s biggest festivals and arenas. Support comes from Mercury Prize-winning indie rockers English Teacher, whose debut album This Could Be Texas has been widely hailed as one of the strongest debuts in recent years. Opening the show are rising Glaswegian punk outfit Humour, rapidly gaining attention across the UK, Europe and the US. This is set to be a huge night in the heart of the city — and one not to miss. 🎟 Tickets Presale opens Thursday 12 February, 10AM General sale from Friday 13 February, 10AM
shortDescription: Scottish rock icons Biffy Clyro headline Cardiff Castle this summer with special guests English Teacher and Humour
subcategory: Biffy Clyro

title: The Streets
description: Mike Skinner and The Streets return to Cardiff for a huge summer show at Cardiff Castle, bringing their iconic album A Grand Don’t Come For Free to life — performed in full, from start to finish, for the very first time. Originally released in 2004, A Grand Don’t Come For Free became one of the most defining British albums of the 2000s. Built around a cinematic storyline that unfolds track by track, the record blends sharp storytelling, humour and raw honesty across classics including Dry Your Eyes, Blinded by the Lights, Fit But You Know It and Could Well Be In. Over two decades later, fans will finally get to experience the full narrative live on stage. Joining the bill as special guest is CASISDEAD, the elusive UK rapper known for his cinematic lyricism and dark, synth-driven sound. Widely regarded as one of the most inventive voices in UK rap, his atmospheric live performances have earned him a devoted following. Opening the night is Jimothy Lacoste, the cult favourite artist whose self-produced tracks and offbeat storytelling have built an international fanbase. With his DIY approach and witty take on everyday life, he brings a unique energy to the lineup. Set within the historic walls of Cardiff Castle, this one-off performance promises a rare chance to experience one of the UK’s most influential albums exactly as it was intended — live, in full, and in an unforgettable setting.
shortDescription: The Streets perform A Grand Don’t Come For Free in full at Cardiff Castle with CASISDEAD and Jimothy Lacoste.
subcategory: The Streets

title: Sex Pistols feat. Frank Carter
description: Punk history lands in the heart of Cardiff as Sex Pistols featuring Frank Carter headline Cardiff Castle on Saturday 1 August 2026, marking 50 years of punk. Steve Jones, Paul Cook and Glen Matlock are joined by Frank Carter for a full-throttle celebration of one of the most influential bands of all time. Fresh off a huge year of global shows and a fiery revival that’s seen circle pits at the Royal Albert Hall and a packed-out return to London’s 100 Club, the Pistols are hitting Cardiff for a massive outdoor show. They’re joined by a heavyweight punk lineup: The Stranglers, The Undertones, and Cardiff’s own rising force Panic Shack opening the night. Expect a relentless, high-energy bill honouring the legacy, attitude and chaos that kicked off a movement in 1976 — brought to life by a band that helped define it. This is a milestone moment for the castle’s summer series and one of the standout shows of 2026.
shortDescription: A huge night of punk at Cardiff Castle as Sex Pistols featuring Frank Carter are joined by The Stranglers, The Undertones and Panic Shack.

title: SOLD OUT: Two Door Cinema Club
description: Indie icons Two Door Cinema Club are celebrating the 15th anniversary of their debut album Tourist History with a huge outdoor show at Cardiff Castle, as part of TK Maxx presents DEPOT Live 2026. The Northern Irish trio – Alex Trimble, Sam Halliday and Kevin Baird – will perform Tourist History in full, alongside fan favourites from across their career. Expect a night of high-energy anthems and pure indie nostalgia, featuring hits like What You Know, Something Good Can Work and Undercover Martyn. Joining them for this special anniversary show are Friendly Fires, whose Mercury-nominated debut album became a defining soundtrack of the late 2000s, and exciting new act 3LIJAH, opening the evening with their gritty, genre-blurring sound. From their breakout in 2010 to headlining major festivals around the world, Tourist History remains a landmark record that continues to resonate – with What You Know recently surpassing 1 billion streams on Spotify. Don’t miss one of the biggest live shows of summer 2026 as Two Door Cinema Club bring their electric live energy to the heart of Cardiff. 🎟️ Tickets Presale: 10am Thursday 30 October General sale: 10am Friday 31 October

title: Hollywood Vampires
description: American rock supergroup Hollywood Vampires return to the UK for their first live shows in almost three years — and they’re bringing one of the loudest nights of the summer to Cardiff Castle. Featuring Alice Cooper, Johnny Depp, Joe Perry and Tommy Henriksen, Hollywood Vampires will headline a huge open-air show in one of the most iconic settings in the country. Known for their explosive live performances, the band deliver a no-holds-barred celebration of rock ’n’ roll, blending original material with blistering tributes to the legends who shaped the genre. Expect stone-cold classics and powerhouse performances inspired by the likes of The Who, Led Zeppelin, David Bowie, Motörhead, AC/DC and more, alongside tracks from the band’s own releases and iconic hits from Alice Cooper and Aerosmith. This is a full-throttle rock show built for big stages and summer nights. Special guests The Damned join the bill, bringing true punk and goth-rock royalty to the castle walls. Celebrating their 50th anniversary, the legendary band boast a career spanning 12 studio albums and some of the most influential records in British punk history, making this a rare and unmissable pairing. Hollywood Vampires at Cardiff Castle promises a loud, electric and unforgettable night of rock history in the making. ➡ Presale – Thursday 22 January at 10AM ➡ General Sale – Friday 23 January at 10AM

description: Jimmy Eat World return to the UK in 2026 for a run of exclusive outdoor shows celebrating 25 years of their landmark album Bleed American — with a huge one-night takeover at TK Maxx presents DEPOT Live at Cardiff Castle. The Arizona alt-rock icons will perform the album in full for the first time in the UK, alongside rarities and fan favourites from across their catalogue. Expect a bigger production than ever as the band mark a record that helped define a generation. They’re joined by a stacked line-up: Chicago punk heavyweights Rise Against as special guests, with The Get Up Kids and Jay Som completing the bill. Bleed American remains a pivotal release — certified Platinum in the US, Gold in the UK, and home to era-defining tracks like The Middle, which has surpassed a billion streams. Its influence runs deep, shaping a wave of modern rock and alternative artists. This Cardiff Castle date sits within a blockbuster summer of shows for DEPOT Live, joining a packed 2026 programme of major artists and large-scale outdoor events. A milestone album. A rare full-album performance. A standout night in the castle. 🎟 Tickets Presale opens Thursday 4 December, 10AM General sale from Friday 5 December, 10AM

description: The History of Jungle brings its UK tour to Depot this September, with a lineup drawn from the defining era of UK jungle. Ten DJs. Eight MCs. One sound system built to carry the music properly. Cardiff has its own deep relationship with this music. From early sound system culture in Butetown through to the rise of jungle and drum and bass, the city built its scene through community, independent spaces and a DIY spirit that sits at the very heart of what jungle is. This is a natural home for an event like this. title: I Prevail – The Violent Nature Tour
description: Grammy-nominated metalcore heavyweights I Prevail bring The Violent Nature Tour to DEPOT Cardiff this September, following phenomenal demand across the UK. This extra Cardiff date promises an intense, full-throttle night as the band showcase tracks from their explosive new album Violent Nature alongside fan favourites from Lifelines, TRAUMA and TRUE POWER. Formed in Metro Detroit in 2013, I Prevail have grown from underground hopefuls to one of modern heavy music’s most powerful live acts, earning gold-certified records on both sides of the Atlantic and a Grammy nomination for Best Rock Album along the way. Violent Nature pushes them even further, channelling personal conflict and raw emotion into some of their heaviest, most cathartic songs to date – from the ferocious title track to the huge, melodic hooks of “Rain”. Joining them in Cardiff are special guests Polaris, Amira Elfeky, and Gore. setting the tone with a stacked bill of crushing riffs, soaring choruses and big, emotional singalongs from start to finish. Expect a high-energy, no-compromise show from a band entering a new era – louder, darker and more ambitious than ever.

description: Following a run of sold-out shows throughout 2025, Autechre return in 2026 with an expanded run of live dates. Sean Booth and Rob Brown will head from Japan in February into a major European stretch across September and October — including their biggest headline show to date in London — before landing in Cardiff this October. Autechre’s live shows are unlike anything else: full immersion, complete focus, and a performance delivered entirely in darkness. This is a rare chance to experience one of electronic music’s most influential and uncompromising acts in their purest form. 🎟️ Tickets ➡ Presale live Wednesday 3rd December, 11am ➡ General sale Thursday 4th December, 11am DISCLAIMER: Autechre will perform in total darkness. Please do not shine any lights at the stage or around the room during the performance. If you need to leave for any reason please use your mobile phone to light your way. Absolutely no recording or flash photography please. If you are uncomfortable with the idea of spending around one hour in the dark while Autechre play please do not book a ticket for this concert. Thanks and enjoy the experience, Autechre

title: Little Angels – Big Bad & Back Tour
description: Legendary UK hard rock band Little Angels are Big, Bad & Back — returning with an ultra-rare UK tour featuring the full original line-up. Formed nearly 40 years ago in Scarborough, Little Angels became one of the defining British rock bands of the late ’80s and early ’90s, building a fiercely loyal fanbase and scoring 11 UK Top 40 singles along the way. Their 1993 album Jam hit #1, cementing their place at the top of the UK rock scene. The last time the band toured was in 2012, following their triumphant return at Download Festival. Before that? A sold-out farewell show at London’s Royal Albert Hall in 1994. Now, for the first time in over a decade, Toby Jepson, Bruce Dickinson, Jim Dickinson, Mark Plunkett and Mark Richardson reunite for a proper return — not a nostalgia cash-in, but a celebration of an era, a scene, and a band that helped define it. Joining them as special guest on all dates is long-time friend and Thunder guitarist Luke Morley, making this a night firmly rooted in one of the most iconic periods of UK hard rock. 🎟️ TICKETS On sale 18 February at 9am

title: Midge Ure – A Man Of Two Worlds
description: Acclaimed musician, producer, and Ultravox frontman Midge Ure brings his visionary A Man Of Two Worlds Tour to DEPOT, Cardiff on 25 November 2026. This bold new live concept offers fans a rare and deeply personal concert experience. For the first time, Ure will blend his iconic hits and fan favourites with the atmospheric instrumentals that have long featured on his solo albums — many of which have never been performed live. The result is a rich show designed to take the audience on an emotional journey, both visually and sonically. From his early days with Slik and The Rich Kids, to defining the sound of the ’80s with Ultravox and co-creating the global charity single Do They Know It’s Christmas?, Midge Ure has always challenged musical expectations. With Ivor Novello, Grammy and BASCAP awards to his name, he continues to push boundaries with this ambitious new show. Don’t miss the chance to experience a truly unique evening of music that bridges decades of creativity — a live set not just performed, but curated, woven together into an unforgettable narrative. THIS IS A SEATED ONLY EVNET TICKETS ➡️ Presale – Weds 11th March, 9.30am ➡️ General sale – Fri 13th March, 9.30am

title: James B Partridge presents The Big Christmas Assembly
description: Join James B Partridge for The Big Christmas Assembly 2026 – the nostalgic, feel-good festive singalong that brings your childhood Christmases flooding back. Packed with more festive classics, throwback mashups, and laugh-out-loud stories than you can throw a glitter-glue halo at. What began as playful performances for his pupils has grown into a nationwide phenomenon – from TikTok fame to closing the field at Glastonbury Festival. With recent appearances on Virgin Radio, BBC Radio 2, The One Show and Songs of Praise, James has never been more in demand – and with the majority of his 2025 Christmas tour selling out well in advance, early booking is strongly advised. Whether you’re organising the work Christmas party, planning that festive night out with friends, or simply treating yourself to the perfect pre-Christmas warm-up – this is guaranteed to get you, and everyone around you, well and truly in the spirit. Mulled wine in hand, the whole room singing Calypso Carol like it’s the last day of term – the kind of communal magic that lingers all the way to Boxing Day. James says: “I’ve always loved how the songs we sang as children at Christmas brought so much joy. This show is a celebration of those moments – from assembly halls to nativity plays – and I can’t wait to see audiences relive them together.”
shortDescription: The nostalgic, feel-good festive singalong packed with more Christmas classics & throwback mashups than you can throw a glitter-glue halo.

Expect a packed set featuring music made famous by the likes of Shania Twain, Luke Combs, Carrie Underwood, Taylor Swift and Chris Stapleton, plus iconic tributes to legends including Dolly Parton, Johnny Cash and Kenny Rogers. Fronted by UK country star Maria Jordan and backed by a powerhouse live band, the show transports you straight to downtown Nashville with big vocals, standout musicianship and wall-to-wall singalongs. description:
The Cycle Continues... 
Bandito’s we need your help, we need to find Clancy.
As you know last year during the City Walls battle, we lost Clancy. And in the words of Torchbearer:
“That’s not Clancy up there anymore. He’s out there somewhere. And we will try again.”
So now it is time for us, to try again.
We are gathering the Cardiff Bandito’s at Clwb Ifor Bach on May 8th and together we will try to find Clancy.
You are encouraged to dress from your favourite twenty one pilots era and please bring with you, your voice and your energy.
Our aim is to play for you every song from every Twenty One Pilots album over the course of the 4 hour gathering. In order for this mission to succeed, we need to move fast. With your energy, your voices and your help, together we might just be able to do this.
And remember, the sun will rise, and we will try again.
Always.
